builtins.title: Builtins
builtins.description: Functions supplied directly by the compiler and VM runtime.

modules.title: Global functions
modules.description: Language-level builtins and default output functions available without an import.

functions.name: len
functions.description: Return the length of a string, array, or map.

functions.name: slice
functions.description: Slice a string from the given start and length.
signatures: fn slice(text: string, start: int, length: int) -> string
fn slice(items: array, start: int, length: int) -> array
fn slice(items: bytes, start: int, length: int) -> bytes

functions.name: concat
functions.description: Concatenate two strings.
signatures: fn concat(left: string, right: string) -> string
fn concat(left: array, right: array) -> array
fn concat(left: bytes, right: bytes) -> bytes

functions.name: array_new
functions.description: Create an empty array.

functions.name: array_push
functions.description: Append a value to an array and return the updated array.
signatures: fn array_push(items: array, value: any) -> array

functions.name: map_new
functions.description: Create an empty map.

functions.name: get
functions.description: Read a string entry.
signatures: fn get(text: string, index: int) -> string
fn get(items: array, index: int) -> unknown
fn get(items: bytes, index: int) -> int
fn get(entries: map, key: any) -> unknown

functions.name: has
functions.description: Check whether an array contains a valid index.
signatures: fn has(items: array, index: int) -> bool
fn has(items: bytes, index: int) -> bool
fn has(entries: map, key: any) -> bool

functions.name: type
functions.description: Return the runtime type name of a value.

functions.name: set
functions.description: Update an array entry and return the updated array.
signatures: fn set(items: array, index: int, value: any) -> array
fn set(entries: map, key: any, value: any) -> map

functions.name: keys
functions.description: Return an array of container keys or indices.

functions.name: count
functions.description: Return the number of entries in an array or map.

functions.name: assert
functions.description: Abort execution if the condition is false.

functions.name: string_contains
functions.description: Return true when `needle` is found within `text`.
signatures: fn string_contains(text: string, needle: string) -> bool

functions.name: string_replace_literal
functions.description: Replace non-overlapping literal `needle` matches in `text`.
signatures: fn string_replace_literal(text: string, needle: string, replacement: string) -> string

functions.name: string_lower_ascii
functions.description: Lower ASCII `A`-`Z` bytes in `text` while preserving UTF-8.

functions.name: string_split_literal
functions.description: Split `text` on non-overlapping literal `delimiter` matches.
signatures: fn string_split_literal(text: string, delimiter: string) -> array

functions.name: print
functions.description: Writes a value to the runtime print sink.

functions.name: println
functions.description: Writes a value to the runtime print sink and appends a newline.

functions.name: from_utf8
functions.description: Encodes a string as UTF-8 bytes.

functions.name: to_utf8
functions.description: Decodes UTF-8 bytes into a string.

functions.name: to_utf8_lossy
functions.description: Decodes bytes into a string using UTF-8 replacement semantics.

functions.name: from_hex
functions.description: Decodes a hexadecimal string into bytes.

functions.name: to_hex
functions.description: Encodes bytes as lowercase hexadecimal.

functions.name: from_base64
functions.description: Decodes a base64 string into bytes.

functions.name: to_base64
functions.description: Encodes bytes as standard base64.

functions.name: from_array_u8
functions.description: Converts an array of ints in 0..=255 into bytes.

functions.name: to_array_u8
functions.description: Converts bytes into an array of ints in 0..=255.

functions.name: open
functions.description: Opens a file handle for runtime I/O.
signatures: fn open(path: string, mode: string) -> int

functions.name: popen
functions.description: Starts a child process and returns a process-backed handle.
signatures: fn popen(command: string, mode: string) -> int

functions.name: read_all
functions.description: Reads all remaining text from an I/O handle.

functions.name: read_line
functions.description: Reads a single line of text from an I/O handle.

functions.name: write
functions.description: Writes text to an I/O handle.
signatures: fn write(handle_id: int, text: string) -> int

functions.name: flush
functions.description: Flushes buffered output for an I/O handle.

functions.name: close
functions.description: Closes an I/O handle.

functions.name: exists
functions.description: Returns whether a file system path exists.

functions.name: match
functions.description: Returns whether a regular expression matches the input text.
signatures: fn match(pattern: string, text: string) -> bool

functions.name: find
functions.description: Returns the first substring matched by a regular expression.
signatures: fn find(pattern: string, text: string) -> string

functions.name: replace
functions.description: Replaces all regular-expression matches in a string.
signatures: fn replace(pattern: string, text: string, replacement: string) -> string

functions.name: split
functions.description: Splits a string on regular-expression matches.
signatures: fn split(pattern: string, text: string) -> array

functions.name: captures
functions.description: Returns the capture groups produced by the first regular-expression match.
signatures: fn captures(pattern: string, text: string) -> array

functions.name: encode
functions.description: Encodes a `Value` into a JSON string.

functions.name: decode
functions.description:
Decodes a JSON string into a `Value`.
Note: This function enforces a strict unique-keys contract for JSON objects.
If the JSON string contains duplicate keys, it will return an error instead of letting the last key win.

functions.name: set_config
functions.description: Sets the JIT runtime configuration from a map.
signatures: fn set_config(enabled: bool, hot_loop_threshold: int, max_trace_len: int) -> map

functions.name: get_config
functions.description: Returns the current JIT runtime configuration as a map.

functions.name: set_enabled
functions.description: Enables or disables the JIT at runtime.

functions.name: get_enabled
functions.description: Returns whether the JIT is enabled at runtime.

functions.name: set_hot_loop_threshold
functions.description: Sets the loop-hotness threshold used by the JIT.

functions.name: get_hot_loop_threshold
functions.description: Returns the loop-hotness threshold used by the JIT.

functions.name: set_max_trace_len
functions.description: Sets the maximum trace length used by the JIT.

functions.name: get_max_trace_len
functions.description: Returns the maximum trace length used by the JIT.

functions.description: Returns whether a number is NaN.

functions.description: Raises a number to a floating-point power.

functions.description: Returns the hypotenuse length for two numbers.

modules.title: runtime
modules.description: Default controls for sleeping and terminating the current VM invocation.

functions.name: sleep
functions.description: Sleeps for the requested milliseconds.

functions.name: exit
functions.description: Halts the current VM invocation immediately.

stdlibs.title: Stdlibs
stdlibs.description: Pure RustScript modules shipped with the runtime.

modules.title: bytes
modules.description: Byte-sequence slicing, comparison, searching, and integer encoding helpers.

functions.name: slice
functions.description: Returns a byte slice.
signatures: pub fn slice(buf: bytes, start: int, len: int) -> bytes

functions.name: equal
functions.description: Returns whether two byte sequences are equal.
signatures: pub fn equal(lhs: bytes, rhs: bytes) -> bool

functions.name: starts_with
functions.description: Returns whether buf starts with prefix.
signatures: pub fn starts_with(buf: bytes, prefix: bytes) -> bool

functions.name: ends_with
functions.description: Returns whether buf ends with suffix.
signatures: pub fn ends_with(buf: bytes, suffix: bytes) -> bool

functions.name: index_of
functions.description: Returns the first index of needle in buf, or -1 when it is absent.
signatures: pub fn index_of(buf: bytes, needle: bytes) -> int

functions.name: push_u8
functions.description: Appends a single u8 value to a byte sequence.
signatures: pub fn push_u8(buf: bytes, value: int) -> bytes

functions.name: read_u8
functions.description: Reads a u8 value at the given offset.
signatures: pub fn read_u8(buf: bytes, offset: int) -> int

functions.name: read_u16_be
functions.description: Reads a big-endian u16 at the given offset.
signatures: pub fn read_u16_be(buf: bytes, offset: int) -> int

functions.name: read_u16_le
functions.description: Reads a little-endian u16 at the given offset.
signatures: pub fn read_u16_le(buf: bytes, offset: int) -> int

functions.name: write_u16_be
functions.description: Encodes a big-endian u16 value.

functions.name: write_u16_le
functions.description: Encodes a little-endian u16 value.

functions.name: index_of
functions.description: Returns the first index of a value in an array, or -1 when it is absent.
signatures: pub fn index_of<T>(values: [T], needle: T) -> int

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Returns whether an array contains a value.
signatures: pub fn contains<T>(values: [T], needle: T) -> bool

functions.name: reverse
functions.description: Returns a reversed copy of an array.

functions.name: dedup
functions.description: Returns an array with duplicate values removed.

functions.name: has
functions.description: Returns whether a map contains a key.
signatures: pub fn has<K, V>(map: map<V>, key: K) -> bool

functions.name: get_or
functions.description: Returns a map value or a fallback when the key is absent.
signatures: pub fn get_or<K, V>(map: map<V>, key: K, fallback: V) -> V

functions.name: values
functions.description: Returns the values from a map as an array.

functions.name: entries
functions.description: Returns the [key, value] pairs from a map as an array.
signatures: pub fn entries<K, V>(map: map<V>) -> [[K, V]]

functions.name: merge
functions.description: Merges two maps, preferring values from rhs for duplicate keys.
signatures: pub fn merge<V>(lhs: map<V>, rhs: map<V>) -> map<V>

modules.title: io
modules.description: High-level text file operations built on the native `io` namespace.

functions.name: read_text
functions.description: Reads an entire file into a string.

functions.name: write_text
functions.description: Writes text to a file, replacing any existing contents.
signatures: pub fn write_text(path: string, text: string) -> int

functions.name: append_text
functions.description: Appends text to the end of a file.
signatures: pub fn append_text(path: string, text: string) -> int

functions.name: read_lines
functions.description: Reads a file and splits it into newline-delimited lines.

modules.title: iter
modules.description: Range, collection traversal, transformation, filtering, and reduction helpers.

functions.name: range_step
functions.description: Returns an array of numbers from start to stop using the given step.
signatures: pub fn range_step(start: int, stop: int, step: int) -> [int]

functions.name: range
functions.description: Returns an array of numbers from 0 up to stop.

functions.name: enumerate
functions.description: Returns [index, value] pairs for an array.

functions.name: zip
functions.description: Zips two arrays into [lhs, rhs] pairs up to the shorter length.
signatures: pub fn zip<L, R>(lhs: [L], rhs: [R]) -> [[L, R]]

functions.name: take
functions.description: Returns the first count values from an array.

functions.name: skip
functions.description: Returns an array without the first count values.

functions.name: chunk
functions.description: Splits an array into fixed-size chunks.

functions.name: sum
functions.description: Returns the sum of numeric values.

functions.name: any_true
functions.description: Returns whether any value in an array is truthy.

functions.name: all_true
functions.description: Returns whether every value in an array is truthy.

functions.name: map
functions.description: Applies a function to each value in an array.
signatures: pub fn map<T, U>(values: [T], func: fn(T) -> U) -> [U]

functions.name: filter
functions.description: Returns the values for which pred(value) is true.
signatures: pub fn filter<T>(values: [T], pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> [T]

functions.name: reduce
functions.description: Reduces an array by applying func(acc, value) from left to right.
signatures: pub fn reduce<T, U>(values: [T], init: U, func: fn(U, T) -> U) -> U

functions.name: each
functions.description: Calls a function for each value in an array and returns null.
signatures: pub fn each<T, U>(values: [T], func: fn(T) -> U) -> null

modules.title: lrucache
modules.description: A generic least-recently-used cache implemented in pure RustScript.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Creates an LRU cache with the given capacity.
signatures: pub fn new<K, V>(raw_capacity: int) -> LruCacheState<K, V>

functions.name: capacity
functions.description: Returns the cache capacity.
signatures: pub fn capacity<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> int

functions.name: len
functions.description: Returns the number of cached entries.
signatures: pub fn len<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> int

functions.name: is_empty
functions.description: Returns whether the cache is empty.
signatures: pub fn is_empty<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> bool

functions.name: has
functions.description: Returns whether the cache contains a key.
signatures: pub fn has<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K) -> bool

functions.name: peek
functions.description: Returns the cached value without updating recency.
signatures: pub fn peek<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K) -> LruPeekResult<V>

functions.name: get
functions.description: Returns the cached value and promotes it to the most recently used position.
signatures: pub fn get<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K) -> LruGetResult<K, V>

functions.name: put
functions.description: Inserts or updates a cache entry and returns the next cache state.
signatures: pub fn put<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K, value: V) -> LruCacheState<K, V>

functions.name: touch
functions.description: Promotes a key to the most recently used position if it exists.
signatures: pub fn touch<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K) -> LruCacheState<K, V>

functions.name: remove
functions.description: Removes a cache entry by key.
signatures: pub fn remove<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>, key: K) -> LruCacheState<K, V>

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Removes all entries from the cache.
signatures: pub fn clear<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> LruCacheState<K, V>

functions.name: keys
functions.description: Returns cache keys from oldest to newest.
signatures: pub fn keys<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> [K]

functions.name: values
functions.description: Returns cache values from oldest to newest.
signatures: pub fn values<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> [V]

functions.name: entries
functions.description: Returns cache entries from oldest to newest.
signatures: pub fn entries<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> [LruEntryRow<K, V>]

functions.name: oldest
functions.description: Returns the oldest cache entry report.
signatures: pub fn oldest<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> LruEntryReport<K, V>

functions.name: newest
functions.description: Returns the newest cache entry report.
signatures: pub fn newest<K, V>(cache: LruCacheState<K, V>) -> LruEntryReport<K, V>

types.name: LruCacheState
types.description: Opaque cache state passed to and returned by cache operations.
types.declaration:
struct LruCacheState<K, V> {
    capacity: int,
    size: int,
    next_id: int,
    head: int,
    tail: int,
    lookup: map<int>,
    nodes: map<LruNode<K, V>>
}

types.name: LruPeekResult
types.description: Result of a non-promoting cache lookup.

types.name: LruGetResult
types.description: Result of a promoting cache lookup, including the updated cache state.

types.name: LruEntryReport
types.description: Optional oldest or newest cache entry.

types.name: LruEntryRow

modules.title: parse
modules.description: Validated integer and boolean parsing helpers.

functions.name: is_int_literal_base
functions.description: Returns whether a string is a valid integer literal for the given base.
signatures: pub fn is_int_literal_base(value: string, base: int) -> bool

functions.name: is_int_literal
functions.description: Returns whether a string is a base-10 integer literal.

functions.name: try_parse_int_base
functions.description: Parses an integer literal for the given base and returns null on failure.
signatures: pub fn try_parse_int_base(value: string, base: int) -> int?

functions.name: try_parse_int
functions.description: Parses a base-10 integer literal and returns null on failure.

functions.name: parse_int_base_or
functions.description: Parses an integer literal for the given base or returns a fallback.
signatures: pub fn parse_int_base_or(value: string, base: int, fallback: int) -> int

functions.description: Parses a base-10 integer literal or returns a fallback.
signatures: pub fn parse_int_or(value: string, fallback: int) -> int

functions.name: is_bool_literal

functions.name: try_parse_bool
functions.description: Parses a boolean literal and returns null on failure.

functions.name: parse_bool_or
functions.description: Parses a boolean literal or returns a fallback.
signatures: pub fn parse_bool_or(value: string, fallback: bool) -> bool

modules.title: path
modules.description: Portable path separator, joining, basename, directory, and extension helpers.

functions.description: Joins two path segments with a single separator.

functions.description: Returns the final path segment.

functions.name: dirname
functions.description: Returns the parent directory portion of a path.

functions.name: extname

signatures: pub fn equals(lhs: string, rhs: string) -> bool

functions.name: is_empty
functions.description: Returns whether a string is empty.

functions.name: non_empty
functions.description: Returns whether a string is not empty.

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Returns whether a string contains a substring.
signatures: pub fn contains(haystack: string, needle: string) -> bool

functions.name: split
functions.description: Splits a string on a separator.
signatures: pub fn split(value: string, separator: string) -> [string]

functions.name: trim
functions.description: Trims leading and trailing ASCII whitespace from a string.

functions.name: replace
functions.description: Replaces all matching substrings in a string.
signatures: pub fn replace(value: string, needle: string, replacement: string) -> string
